prose:
Method provenance and key results

• Property: Kohn–Sham HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-style orbital gap)  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density-fitting (PySCF)  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied O₂ structure (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz); single-point only, no re-optimization (notation: DFT//input geometry).  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 3 (triplet O₂, unrestricted KS).  
• Software: PySCF backend via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill.  
• Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 7 cycles; **no warnings reported** by the engine.  
• Live log: calculation ran with real-time output to the standard chemkit `frontier_<timestamp>.out` log in the working directory (written line-by-line while running); JSON results saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpa1c479ez/live.json.  

Computed orbital energies (eV):  
– HOMO (β, π_g): −8.8739616  
– LUMO (π_g*): −3.5859031  
⇒ HOMO–LUMO gap = **5.2880586 eV**

(These are Kohn–Sham orbital energies; the gap is not an optical excitation energy and should not be compared directly to experimental spectra without further treatment.)
